pattern: Every digit needs its own stack of ones piled up to reach its height, and the tallest digit sets how many piles the whole number needs.
hints: Think of subtracting 1 from every nonzero digit, once per deci-binary number you peel off -- how many rounds does that take?
The number of rounds needed is driven entirely by whichever digit starts out largest, since every other digit reaches zero no later.
You never need to simulate anything: the answer is simply the value of the single largest digit in the string.

edge_inputs.description: single digit at the max value
zeros in the middle must not affect the answer
all digits are 9, still only needs 9 piles

stress.description: random digit string near the length bound, no leading zero
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    length = max(1, size)
    first = str(rng.randint(1, 9))
    rest = ''.join(str(rng.randint(0, 9)) for _ in range(length - 1))
    return [first + rest]
